name: Sustainable investment proportion floor (PB 90%)

regulatory_anchors.article: Article 2(17) (definition of 'sustainable investment'); Article 9(1) (Article 9 products must invest in economic activities that contribute to a sustainable objective)

regulatory_anchors.regulation: ESMA Guidelines on funds' names using ESG or sustainability-related terms
regulatory_anchors.article: Section 2 (sustainable investment proportion thresholds for fund-name eligibility, May 2024)

summary: PB requires a minimum 90% sustainable investment proportion (per SFDR Art 2(17)) for an Article 9 alignment verdict, excluding cash held for liquidity and instruments held purely for hedging. The 90% threshold sits above the converged market median (~80%) and below the literal reading of the Commission's June 2022 Q&A (effectively 100%); selected to preserve a meaningful qualitative gap between Article 8 and Article 9 verdicts and to front-run potential regulatory tightening. Necessary but not sufficient — DNSH, good-governance, and pre-contractual objective tests must independently pass.
successor_regime_note: Under proposed SFDR 2.0 (Commission proposal COM(2025) 841, tabled 2025), the sustainable investment proportion threshold for the highest-tier category is expected to be set at or above 90%; PB's 90% floor was selected partly to align with this anticipated direction. PB Art 9 verdicts issued under methodology v3.3 are stamped to SFDR v1 (Reg 2019/2088 as consolidated 09/01/2024) and remain valid as of date of issue.
